label: Farmers IoT — both sensors
info:
Receives readings from the water tank probe (Module 1) and the soil
probe (Module 2), and serves a live page at GET / so you can watch both
from a phone browser.

Works whether the two sensors are on one ESP8266 or two — each has its
own endpoint, so nothing cares where the POST came from.

SUPERSEDES flows/water-level-flow.json. Do not import both: two `http in`
nodes on /water will conflict.

Core nodes only — no node-red-dashboard install. Every extra install step
is a place a build dies on someone else's phone.

Test with no hardware at all:

    curl -X POST http://localhost:1880/water -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
         -d '{"node":"water-tank-1","ok":true,"raw":139,"depth_mm":113}'

    curl -X POST http://localhost:1880/soil -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
         -d '{"node":"soil-bed-1","ok":true,"moisture_pct":22.4,"temp_c":20.2,"ec":0}'

Then open http://<phone-ip>:1880/ in a browser.

name: READ ME — endpoints, payloads, and the one thing people forget
info:
ENDPOINTS
  POST /water   Module 1, the QDY30A tank probe
  POST /soil    Module 2, the THC-S soil probe
  GET  /        live page showing both

PAYLOADS
  /water  {"node":"water-tank-1","ok":true,"raw":139,"depth_mm":113,
           "percent":47.6,"rssi":-62,"uptime_s":1234}
  /soil   {"node":"soil-bed-1","ok":true,"moisture_pct":22.4,
           "temp_c":20.2,"ec":0,"rssi":-49,"uptime_s":1234}

A failed sensor read still POSTs, with ok:false and an error string. That
is deliberate: a node that goes silent looks exactly like dead WiFi or a
flat battery. An explicit error says the node is alive and the probe is
not, which is a different repair job.

THE ONE THING PEOPLE FORGET: every `http in` needs an `http response`.
Without it the request never completes and the ESP blocks until it times
out. Both are wired here already — don't delete them.

STALENESS: the page shows how long ago each reading arrived and marks it
STALE past 90s. Absence is the failure mode you cannot see otherwise —
a node that stops reporting produces no error anywhere, so the only
symptom is a number that quietly stops changing.
